jobs.title: Sr. Product Manager, International Tech Latam
jobs.jd_full: O Sr. Product Manager (PM) na Amazon trabalha na definição e execução da estratégia para expansão de produtos no Brasil, influenciando a direção da tecnologia que impacta milhões de clientes globalmente. O PM lidera o planejamento e execução do roadmap de produtos para expandir as capacidades da Amazon no Brasil, colaborando com equipes de Tax, Accounting, Legal e Public Policy para garantir que as soluções atendam aos requisitos regulatórios. Responsável pelo desenvolvimento de produtos do início ao fim, incluindo a criação de documentos detalhando requisitos e histórias de usuários para engenharia, gerenciando trade-offs entre ganhos de curto prazo e apostas estratégicas de longo prazo, e desenvolvendo a estratégia de longo prazo para expansões de produtos no Brasil.

jobs.title: Product Manager, LATAM (São Paulo, Brasil)
jobs.jd_full: O Product Manager, LATAM será responsável por liderar a estratégia e execução de produtos para a região da América Latina. Trabalhará em estreita colaboração com equipes multifuncionais para identificar oportunidades de crescimento, definir requisitos de produtos e garantir a entrega bem-sucedida de soluções que atendam às necessidades dos usuários e aos objetivos de negócios. O candidato ideal terá experiência em gerenciamento de produtos, conhecimento profundo do mercado LATAM e habilidades comprovadas em liderança de equipes multifuncionais.

jobs.title: Remote Technical Project Manager (Data Privacy & AI)
jobs.location: Argentina, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Chile, Colombia, Cyprus, Estonia, Georgia, Germany, Hungary, Mexico, Montenegro, Peru, Poland, Portugal, Serbia, Slovakia, Spain, Turkey
jobs.jd_full:
About the Company

Our client is a profitable Enterprise SaaS company specializing in data privacy and security. With a powerful Data Privacy Governance platform that leverages patented AI technology, they are revolutionizing customer data protection for Fortune 100 organizations. This innovative solution ensures seamless compliance with GDPR, CCPA, and other privacy regulations, while offering unified consent and preference management APIs for efficient data handling across multiple channels.

  • Candidate location: Remote (ET work hours)

  • Product: AI-powered privacy compliance and data governance platform

Our client's mission is to contribute to a better society by advocating for proper data protection legislation, responsibly safeguarding customer data, preserving individual privacy rights, and enhancing trust between businesses and consumers through greater transparency.

The Opportunity

As a Technical Project Manager, you will lead the end-to-end implementation of our client’s AI-powered data privacy compliance solutions, working closely with both enterprise customers and internal teams (product, engineering, customer support) to ensure seamless delivery and client satisfaction. You’ll coordinate project activities, manage timelines, and serve as the main point of contact for clients, proactively resolving issues and keeping everyone aligned. Your expertise will help drive successful outcomes and shape the future of privacy technology for leading organizations.

What You'll Do

  • Lead and manage end-to-end product implementations for clients

  • Coordinate daily with clients and development teams, keeping both sides aligned and accountable

  • Oversee and coordinate developer activities in a structured way: track tasks, monitor status, and ensure quality and deadlines are met

  • Act as the main point of contact for delivery, reporting to the VP of Client Delivery

  • Provide product expertise to clients and serve as a trusted advisor during the implementation phase

  • Proactively identify and resolve issues, keeping projects on track

  • Maintain clear and consistent communication with clients, developers, and senior management

  • Create and maintain project tracking documentation and regular status reports

What You Bring

  • 10+ years of working experience. You're a Software Engineer turned Project Manager in a B2B SaaS product environment

  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills for working with both clients at the VP/C-suite level and internal engineering teams

  • Technical knowledge related to SaaS products or cloud-based platforms (coding not required)

  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple SaaS projects

  • Excellent problem-solving, organizational, and time-management skills

  • Experience working with cross-functional teams (product, engineering, support)

  • Bachelor’s Degree or higher

  • Ability and willingness to work ET hours (till 4 pm)

  • Bonus: background in privacy and knowledge of data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.)

Key Success Drivers

  • High ethical standards, honesty, and transparency

  • Proactive, energetic, involved in company life, and provides feedback

  • Customer-centric approach

  • Comfortable working in a fast-changing startup environment

  • Collaborative team player, commitment to data privacy

Why Join?

  • Competitive compensation

  • Flexible remote work environment

  • Ongoing professional development and career growth opportunities

  • Collaborative company culture and a supportive environment for all employees

  • Proven product-market fit with Fortune 100 customers and established sales processes

  • Work alongside industry experts and a supportive leadership team

  • Be part of a mission-driven company that values innovation, transparency, and trust

Interviewing Process

  • HR Screening

  • Level 1 - 30-minute interview with the Hiring Manager (General fit)

  • Level 2 - Interview with the Hiring Manager (Skills Assessment)

  • Reference and Background Checks: conducted after successful interviews

  • Job Offer: provided to the selected candidate

We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity, or expression, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state or local law.

Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Calyxo, Inc. is a medical device company headquartered in Pleasanton, California, USA. The company was founded in 2016 to address the profound need for improved kidney stone treatment. Kidney stone disease is a common, painful condition that consumes vast amounts of healthcare resources each year. Our team is led by executives and investors with a proven track record of commercializing paradigm-shifting devices to meet unmet needs within urology.

Are you ready to change the future of kidney stone treatment? We are seeking high achievers who want to be part of a dynamic team working in a fun, diverse atmosphere.

Summary

The Product Manager brings the voice of the physician and patient into our efforts to ensure Calyxo’s products deliver on our physician and patient experience.  This position focuses on supporting the innovation and sustaining activities for commercial products as well as supporting new launches, informing the long-term roadmap, and development of product demonstration models. It requires creative problem-solving skills, excellent analytical skills, and the ability to be strategically agile in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.   Ideally, this role will have based in the home office, however, given the focus onsupporting commercial product, hybrid and remote options will also be considered.

In This Role, You Will:

  • Commercial Product

    • Serve as the procedural and product champion for the CVAC System (including ancillaries), providing technical and competitive expertise to Sales and cross functional partners.
    • Act as the internal subject matter expert on procedural best practices, synthesizing personal case experience with input from surgeons, sales, clinical specialists, field trainers, product managers, R&D, and clinical development engineers to identify and prioritize procedural education needs.
    • Define, maintain, and evolve standardized procedural narratives, techniques, and “reasons to believe” that clearly articulate product value and differentiation versus competitive technologies.
    • Own the end-to-end strategy and execution of procedural education, including the development and continuous improvement of training content (videos, animations, presentations, digital modules, and clinical collateral). Ensure this content is grounded in adult learning principles, with consistent messaging, visuals, and clinical claims across all channels.
    • Create and maintain Instructions for Use (IFU) documentation in collaboration with R&D, Clinical, and Regulatory partners.
    • Support conceptualization and development of compelling product demonstrations to reinforce best practices and product value propositions.

  • Product Pipeline

    • Support identification of new product opportunities and procedure enhancements based on customer and market needs and develop prioritized recommendations.
    • Provide upstream marketing leadership on product development teams, including the development of product, market, and branding strategies.
    • Bring the customer’s voice into product development through best-in-class, qualitative, and quantitative customer research techniques.

  • Product Launch

  • Support development and execution of comprehensive early user evaluation for new products.

  • Support commercial launch of future products, including R&D, Operations, Finance, Professional Education, Sales, and Marketing.

  • Ensure smooth transition to Commercial Marketing after initial commercial launch

  • Other

    • Perform other duties as assigned.
    • Contribute to the Company culture of being collaborative, respectful, transparent, ethical, efficient, high-achieving, and fun!

Who You Will Report To:

  • Director, Product Management

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s Degree; or equivalent practical experience.
  • At least 5 years of related work experience in Product Management, Marketing, R&D, and/or Project Management, with at least 3 years in the medical device industry.
  • Experience with innovative Class II or Class III medical devices a plus.
  • Creative problem solving, including excellent analytical skills and the ability to take on complex, ambiguous problems by breaking them into smaller components and finding solutions.
  • Ownership mentality with the ability to take ownership of projects and drive towards goals in a fast-growing, start-up environment.
  • Self-motivated and excellent project and time management skills with a passion for learning and making an impact.
  • Proven ability to act as a business partner and effectively communicate internally and externally at all organizational levels.
  • Demonstrated “Field Focused” style through co-traveling with reps, attending and participating in training workshops, etc.
  • Ability to travel ~30% of time

What We Offer:

At Calyxo, you will be part of a knowledgeable, high-achieving, experienced and fun team.  You will work in a diverse work environment with experienced, proven leaders and have an opportunity to shape our company culture.  You will experience constant learning and dynamic challenges to help you grow and be the best version of yourself.

We also offer a compensation plan as follows:

  • Competitive base salary of $155,000 - $170,000
  • Stock options – ownership and a stake in growing a mission-driven company
  • Employee benefits package that includes 401(k), healthcare insurance and paid vacation

Calyxo is deeply committed to fostering an environment where diversity and inclusion are not only valued but also prioritized. We believe a diverse and inclusive community empowers us to act courageously, care deeply, and dream boldly to impact people in big ways. Diverse viewpoints bring diverse capabilities, which strengthen our focus and fuel our growth.

Calyxo is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, seeking to create a welcoming and diverse environment. All applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, veteran status or any other applicable legally protected characteristics

Legal authorization to work in the United States is required. In compliance with federal law, all persons hired will be required to verify their identity and eligibility to work in the United States and to complete the required employment eligibility verification form upon hire.

Disclaimer:

At Calyxo, we prioritize a transparent and structured interview process to ensure the best fit for both our candidates and our team. Please be aware of the following:

  • Structured Interview Process: Our hiring process includes multiple stages of interviews where you will have the opportunity to communicate directly with Calyxo employees. This ensures that you gain a comprehensive understanding of the role and our company culture.
  • Verification of Identity: We do not extend job offers without first meeting candidates, either virtually or in person. This step is crucial to maintain the integrity of our hiring process and to ensure mutual alignment.
  • Beware of Scams: Calyxo will never request sensitive personal information, such as your full name, address, phone number, or identification documents, via email or online forms before an official interview. Calyxo representatives will always contact you using an email format of firstname.lastname@calyxoinc.com. If you receive a request for information from any other domain, please contact us directly at info@calyxoinc.comto verify the legitimacy of the communication.

We appreciate your interest in joining Calyxo and look forward to getting to know you through our official channels.

jobs.title: Product Manager Optimized Checkout Suite Buyer Experience
jobs.jd_full:
• Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About the team

The Optimized Checkout Suite (OCS) Team‘s vision is to abstract all checkout from our users by offering them a suite of end to end optimized surfaces and capabilities that delivers so much outstanding value (revenue, lower cost and profit) and control, that our users would deem themselves as irresponsible to not leverage Stripe’s OCS.

What you’ll do

We’re looking for a product leader to own the buyer experience on Stripe’s Optimized Checkout Suite. In this role you will set the vision, define the strategy, and execute on the roadmap for Stripe to help its users deliver the best checkout experience for the end customer. This will require you to build deep customer empathy with Stripe’s users and their users, propose holistic non-obvious solutions to meet their needs, and to champion those solutions inside and outside of Stripe.

Our ideal Product Manager has extensive experience with checkout experiences and payment integrations, has managed complex products, and is obsessed with solving for user needs. They are technical, able to put themselves in the shoes of developers, but also have an acute attention to detail when it comes to user experiences. They operate well in close partnership with their engineering teams, as well as drive initially ambiguously defined projects across the broader company. Deep payments, experimentation and user experience knowledge is a bonus for this role.

Responsibilities

  • Develop the long-term vision and strategy for Stripe’s OCS buyer experience prioritize accordingly, and create an execution roadmap
  • Deeply understand our users’ needs by spending time with developers, sales, and support, and by observing patterns/trends across their feedback and the market
  • Propose the right experiments to run to ensure we have the safest and most performant checkout on the internet
  • Set the pace, prioritization and execution for the team
  • Collaborate with stakeholders across the organization such as Engineering, Design, Data Science, Marketing, Sales, Account Management, and Operations

Who you are

We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.

Minimum requirements

  • Brings 8+ years of experience in a product management role
  • Has partnered closely with engineers and designers to build products (especially front end surfaces) and complex systems at scale
  • Has an obsession with product quality and an eye for design
  • Has the ability to thrive with a high level of autonomy and responsibility
  • Brings a desire for continuous product improvement and can optimize for shipping a portfolio of small, medium, and large releases
  • Has strong written and verbal communication skills with a talent for precise articulations of customer problems

Preferred qualifications

  • Strong business acumen and comfortable with complex ecosystem and system level problems
  • Experience with payment integrations is a plus
  • ML and experimentation experience

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Adjudication
jobs.jd_full:
Who We Are:

SmithRx is a rapidly growing, venture-backed Health-Tech company.  Our mission is to disrupt the expensive and inefficient Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) sector by building a next-generation drug acquisition platform driven by cutting edge technology, innovative cost saving tools, and best-in-class customer service.  With hundreds of thousands of members onboarded since 2016, SmithRx has a solution that is resonating with clients all across the country.

We pride ourselves for our mission-driven and collaborative culture that inspires our employees to do their best work. We believe that the U.S healthcare system is in need of transformation, and we come to work each day dedicated to making that change a reality. At our core, we are guided by our company values:

  • Integrity: Our purpose guides our actions and gives us confidence in the path ahead. With unwavering honesty and dependability, we embrace the pressure of challenging the old and exemplify ethical leadership to create the new.
  • Courage: We face continuous challenges with grit and resilience. We embrace the discomfort of the unknown by balancing autonomy with empathy, and ownership with vulnerability. We boldly challenge the status quo to keep moving forward—always.
  • Together: The success of SmithRx reflects the strength of our partnerships and the commitment of our team. Our shared values bind us together and make us one. When one falls, we all fall; when one rises, we all rise.

Job Summary:

The Product and Engineering team at SmithRx is developing the next-generation modern pharmacy benefits management (PBM) platform that will change how companies administer and manage pharmacy benefits. Our unified technology platform provides real-time actionable insights that drive cost savings, power clinical services, and result in a brilliant customer experience. A unified technology platform exists nowhere else in the pharmacy benefit ecosystem to programmatically solve widespread deficiencies. SmithRx delivers unmatched service quality and operational efficiencies that exceed all industry standards.

As a Senior Product Manager, you will own the vision, strategy, and execution of our core claims adjudication engine. This role is the backbone of our mission to disrupt the PBM industry, ensuring that every prescription claim is processed with precision and accuracy. You will leverage your deep expertise in NCPDP standards, real-time transaction processing, and pharmacy billing workflows to build and scale our tech-first platform that outpaces legacy PBM systems. As the product leader for claim routing and pricing logic, you will bridge the gap between complex clinical rules and high-performance engineering, guiding cross-functional teams to deliver a seamless, transparent experience for pharmacies, clients, and members.

What you will do:

  • Product Strategy & Vision: Define and drive the multi-year product roadmap for the SmithRx claims adjudication platform, identifying opportunities to enhance processing speed, automation, and transparency while maintaining a highly flexible, cloud-native architecture.
  • Adjudication Logic & Standards: Serve as an internal product Subject Matter Expert (SME) on NCPDP Telecommunication Standards (D.0 and beyond). Translate complex benefit designs, coordination of benefits (COB), and real-time pricing models into scalable, automated logic.
  • End-to-End Product Lifecycle: Lead the full product lifecycle for adjudication features—from initial requirement gathering through engineering sprints and quality assurance—ensuring that new logic deployments do not disrupt high-volume, real-time transaction flows.
  • Cross-Functional Leadership: Partner closely with Engineering, Data Science, Implementation and Operations teams to write detailed technical user stories, manage the adjudication platform backlog, and ensure the engine scales to support complex clinical rules and custom client configurations.
  • System Integrity & Performance: Create, monitor and optimize key performance indicators (KPIs) related to claim success rates, latency, and financial accuracy. Develop robust testing frameworks to ensure that pricing logic and eligibility checks are 100% accurate at the point of sale.
  • Stakeholder & Vendor Engagement: Collaborate with internal Operations and Clinical teams to streamline manual overrides, exception handling and other processes. Support engagement with external partners, such as switch vendors and PSAOs, to resolve routing issues and improve the pharmacy provider experience.
  • Regulatory & Market Alignment: Ensure the adjudication engine is built to support evolving industry requirements; prior experience working with EGWP guidelines, Medicare Part D compliance, or OIG/CMS reporting standards is a significant plus to ensure our platform remains competitive.

What you will bring to SmithRx:

  • 5+ years of progressive experience in Product Management or a related technical domain, with a minimum of 3+ years dedicated to claims adjudication, pharmacy switch technology, or PBM core platforms.
  • Deep technical knowledge of NCPDP Telecommunication Standards (specifically Version D.0) and the transactional workflows between pharmacies, switches, and PBMs.
  • Expertise in complex benefit strings, including real-time pricing logic, Coordination of Benefits (COB), and eligibility verification.
  • Proven ability to lead cross-functional collaboration across Engineering, Data Science, and Operations to deliver high-concurrency, low-latency technical solutions.
  • Strong analytical skills with experience leveraging AI tools and data modeling to improve system efficiency, audit claim accuracy, and automate exception handling.
  • High-stakes delivery: Ability to prioritize and execute in a fast-paced environment where system uptime and financial accuracy are mission-critical.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the unique ability to translate claim logic into clear business impact for executive leadership and clients.
  • Significant plus: experience incorporating EGWP guidelines into adjudication logic; a strong understanding of CMS-mandated benefit phases and their technical implementation

Why This Role is Exciting:

  • Build the Engine of Innovation – Drive the next wave of PBM disruption by owning the core “brain” of our platform. You’ll build high-performance logic that processes millions of transactions with precision and absolute financial integrity.
  • High-Impact Domain and Ownership – You aren’t just managing a feature; you are shaping the very foundation of how SmithRx scales.
  • A Strategic Seat at the Table – As the primary SME for Claims Adjudication, you won’t just execute; you will define the architecture of our competitive advantage.
  • Solve the “Hard” Problems – Partner with world-class Engineers and Data Scientists to solve the industry’s most complex puzzles.
  • Grow With Us – As SmithRx continues its rapid growth, so will your influence. You will have the opportunity to lead the expansion of our technical product suite and redefine what a modern adjudication platform looks like.

What SmithRx Offers You:

  • Highly competitive wellness benefits including Medical, Pharmacy, Dental, Vision, and Life Insurance and AD&D Insurance
  • Flexible Spending Benefits
  • 401(k) Retirement Savings Program
  • Short-term and long-term disability
  • Discretionary Paid Time Off
  • Paid Company Holidays
  • Wellness Benefits
  • Commuter Benefits
  • Paid Parental Leave benefits
  • Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
  • Well-stocked kitchen in office locations
  • Professional development and training opportunities

jobs.title: Insurance Product Analyst
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

  • Help protect over 5 million Australians, making a real difference in their lives during their most challenging times.
  • 12-month fixed term contract
  • Sydney, Melbourne, or Brisbane office locations

Why Join Us?

Welcome to TAL. As a leading life insurer, we’ve been protecting Australians for over 150 years. Backed by Daiichi Life, we’re driven by big ambitions and empower to create better products and services.

Together with our Partners, we’re helping millions of Australians live a life filled with choices, options, and freedoms. See the direct impact you make delivering support and financial security with care and expertise. Grow beyond expectations with diverse roles, global connections, and exclusive learning opportunities.

Work with passionate, bright and capable colleagues. Feel inspired by supportive leaders. Collaborate with heart, where flexibility, wellbeing and inclusivity is valued. Together, we’re reimagining insurance.

So, bring a curious mind and an ambition to help us become the progressive, digitally enabled leading insurer.

Job Description

As an Insurance Product Analyst, you’ll help shape and steward TAL’s Group Life product portfolio, ensuring our offerings remain competitive, relevant, and deeply aligned to the needs of our partners and their members. Your work strengthens TAL’s mission to support Australians’ financial wellbeing by guiding thoughtful product evolution and enabling sustainable growth. This role is meaningful because the insights you generate and the decisions you influence directly contribute to protecting more Australians and helping them live life with confidence.

In This Role You Will:

  • Drive clear product strategies that strengthen the performance and competitiveness of the portfolio.
  • Negotiate and shape complex contracts to secure strong, sustainable partner outcomes.
  • Ensure products meet legislative and regulatory obligations to protect TAL and its partners.
  • Maintain accurate, compelling product documentation that supports partner confidence and compliance.
  • Analyse market insights and competitor activity to guide strategic product decisions.
  • Implement product enhancements that improve value, experience, and commercial performance.
  • Identify emerging opportunities and risks to inform proactive product evolution.

Please note, this is a 12-month fixed term contract.

Qualifications

  • Tertiary qualifications in law, finance, business, commerce and/or related disciples
  • Apply sound product and insurance knowledge to make clear, well‑reasoned decisions.
  • Demonstrate a solid understanding of compliance and regulatory expectations.
  • Use strong analytical thinking to turn data into meaningful insights and recommendations.
  • Communicate clearly and organise work effectively to deliver quality outcomes.
  • Assess new opportunities with a commercial mindset and a focus on customer value.
  • Build constructive relationships and influence others through collaboration and trust.

Additional Information

TAL is one of Australia’s leading life insurers, committed to inclusion, and supporting the career growth of our diverse workforce. We’re proud to be:

  • An Inclusive Employer – Recognised as Employer of Choice for Gender Equality by the Workplace Gender Equality Agency and Bronze Tier Status within the Australian Workplace Equality Index

  • Diversity Champions – Member of Diversity Council Australia, Australian Disability Network, Pride in Diversity and Champions of Change

  • Reconciliation Advocates – Read our Innovate Reconciliation Action Plan.

  • We welcome applications from people with diverse experiences, perspectives and backgrounds including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, caregivers, individuals living with disabilities, people from culturally diverse backgrounds and the LGBTIQ+ community.

  • Need adjustments during the recruitment process? Let our team know by getting in touch with us here.—we’re here to support you.

You’re always accountable for your actions. You never give up. You strive to find the best outcomes for customers and partners. And you value working together to find the best solutions for problems.

As part of the recruitment process, there are several checks which may be conducted to demonstrate your eligibility for a role at TAL including Criminal History, Bankruptcy, Entitlement to Work, Regulatory and Reference Checks.

#LI-Hybrid

Everyone at TAL has a responsibility to do the right thing and is accountable for the way they conduct themselves. Our expectations are that you follow the principles set out in our Code of Conduct when you come to work every day. Risk management is everyone’s responsibility.

If you are already a TAL employee please apply via the SmartRecruiters button in Workday and navigate to the Employee Portal. This is important to ensure that your application is recorded accurately.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Growth
